summary: Governance layer for AI agents, MCP tools, permissions, and audit trails.
editor_note: The useful angle is not another agent gateway; it is permissioning for the messy agents employees already bring to work. Willow maps agents, tools, MCP servers, identities, policies, and audit trails across 1000+ connectors. Unlike routing-only gateways, it tries to govern what an agent can do inside each tool. The risk is whether teams accept a control layer before shadow AI becomes painful enough.

product_evidence.claim: The official site shows discovery for AI agents, tools, and MCP servers, plus shadow AI detection and usage analytics.
Willow describes build/runtime guards, permissioned tool access, audit trails, IdP connection, Slack/Jira/GitHub connectors, and API-to-MCP conversion.

market_context.primary_user: Security, IT, compliance, and AI-platform teams letting employees use autonomous agents at work.
market_context.differentiation: Willow governs agent identity, tool permissions, MCP access, and auditability rather than only routing agent traffic.
market_context.why_now: The June 2026 seed round lands as enterprises discover unmanaged agents and need enforceable controls beyond policy documents.

risks.claim: The open question is whether teams adopt a control layer early enough, before unmanaged agent usage creates a visible incident.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
